opinion.h4: JENKINS v. BIRZGALIS, STATE HOSPITAL SUPERINTENDENT, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF MICHIGAN. No. 1495, Misc. Decided June 6, 1966.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
384 U.S. 435 DAUGHERTY v. TENNESSEE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TENNESSEE. No. 1435, Misc. Decided June 6, 1966.
216 Tenn. 666, 393 S.W.2d 739, appeal dismissed and certiorari denied.
George F. McCanless, Attorney General of Tennessee, and Thomas E. Fox, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
